children.heading: Appliance
children.label: 1 Appliance
children.text: The term “appliance” means any device which contains and uses a class I or class II substance as a refrigerant and which is used for household or commercial purposes, including any air conditioner, refrigerator, chiller, or freezer.

children.label: A
children.text: the calendar year 1986, in the case of any class I substance listed in Group I or II under ,section 7671a(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the calendar year 1989, in the case of any class I substance listed in Group III, IV, or V under , andsection 7671a(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: any substance added to the list of class I substances after the publication of the initial list under , andsection 7671a(a) of this title

children.heading: Class I substance
children.label: 3 Class I substance
children.text: The term “class I substance” means each of the substances listed as provided in .section 7671a(a) of this title

children.heading: Class II substance
children.label: 4 Class II substance
children.text: The term “class II substance” means each of the substances listed as provided in .section 7671a(b) of this title

children.heading: Commissioner
children.label: 5 Commissioner
children.text: The term “Commissioner” means the Commissioner of the Food and Drug Administration.

children.heading: Consumption
children.label: 6 Consumption
children.text: The term “consumption” means, with respect to any substance, the amount of that substance produced in the United States, plus the amount imported, minus the amount exported to Parties to the Montreal Protocol. Such term shall be construed in a manner consistent with the Montreal Protocol.

children.heading: Import
children.label: 7 Import
children.text: The term “import” means to land on, bring into, or introduce into, or attempt to land on, bring into, or introduce into, any place subject to the jurisdiction of the United States, whether or not such landing, bringing, or introduction constitutes an importation within the meaning of the customs laws of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: if such device, product, drug, or drug delivery system utilizes a class I or class II substance for which no safe and effective alternative has been developed, and where necessary, approved by the Commissioner; and

children.label: B
children.text: if such device, product, drug, or drug delivery system, has, after notice and opportunity for public comment, been approved and determined to be essential by the Commissioner in consultation with the Administrator.

children.heading: Montreal Protocol
children.label: 9 Montreal Protocol
children.text: The terms “Montreal Protocol” and “the Protocol” mean the Montreal Protocol on Substances that Deplete the Ozone Layer, a protocol to the Vienna Convention for the Protection of the Ozone Layer, including adjustments adopted by Parties thereto and amendments that have entered into force.

children.heading: Ozone-depletion potential
children.label: 10 Ozone-depletion potential
children.text: The term “ozone-depletion potential” means a factor established by the Administrator to reflect the ozone-depletion potential of a substance, on a mass per kilogram basis, as compared to chlorofluorocarbon-11 (CFC–11). Such factor shall be based upon the substance’s atmospheric lifetime, the molecular weight of bromine and chlorine, and the substance’s ability to be photolytically disassociated, and upon other factors determined to be an accurate measure of relative ozone-depletion potential.

children.label: A
children.text: the manufacture of a substance that is used and entirely consumed (except for trace quantities) in the manufacture of other chemicals, or

children.label: B
children.text: the reuse or recycling of a substance.
